<content>
1: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
2: % 1. Grid dimensions and helpers
3: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
4: n_rows(Nr) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
5: n_cols(Nc) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
6: 
7: row(R) :- R >= 0, R < Nr.
8: col(C) :- C >= 0, C < Nc.
9: 
10: output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).
11: 
12: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
13: % 2. Find the maximal nonzero colour
14: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
15: maxc(K) :- K = #max{ Color : color(Color), Color != 0 }.
16: 
17: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
18: % 3. Cells that contain the maximal colour
19: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
20: maxcRow(R) :- input(R, _, V), V = maxc.
21: maxcCol(C) :- input(_, C, V), V = maxc.
22: 
23: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
24: % 4. Bounding rectangle for the maximal colour
25: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
26: rStart0 :- rStart0 = #min{ R : maxcRow(R) }.
27: rEnd0 :- rEnd0 = #max{ R : maxcRow(R)} - 2.
28: cStart0 :- cStart0 = #min{ C : maxcCol(C) }.
29: cEnd0 :- cEnd0 = #max{ C : maxcCol(C)} - 2.
30: 
31: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
32: % 5. Inrange predicate (the rectangular area where we may place maxc)
33: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
34: in_range(R,C) :- row(R), col(C),
35:                 le(R, rEnd0), ge(R, rStart0),
36:                 le(C, cEnd0), ge(C, cStart0).
37: 
38: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
39: % 6. Three corner cells that must stay zero
40: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
41: corner(R,C) :- eq(R, rStart0), eq(C, cStart0).
42: corner(R,C) :- eq(R, rStart0), eq(C, cEnd0).
43: corner(R,C) :- eq(R, rEnd0), eq(C, cStart0).
44: 
45: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
46: % 6a. Cells that should receive the maximal colour
47: %      either on a maximalrow (and not a corner)
48: %      or on a maximalcolumn (and not a corner)
49: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
50: targetCell1(R,C) :- maxcRow(R), in_range(R,C), not corner(R,C).
51: targetCell2(R,C) :- maxcCol(C), in_range(R,C), not corner(R,C).
52: 
53: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
54: % 7. Cells that must be zero (neither targetCell1 nor targetCell2)
55: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
56: notTargetCell(R,C) :- not targetCell1(R,C), not targetCell2(R,C).
57: 
58: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
59: % 8. Exactly one colour per output cell
60: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
61: 1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
62: 
63: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
64: % 9. Enforce the required colour
65: % ----------------------------------------------------------------------
66: %    maxc on cells that belong to targetCell1 or targetCell2
67: :- output(R,C,Color), targetCell1(R,C), neq(Color, maxc).
68: :- output(R,C,Color), targetCell2(R,C), neq(Color, maxc).
69: 
70: %    zero on all other cells
72: :- output(R,C,Color), notTargetCell(R,C), neq(Color, 0).
73: 
74: #show output/3.
</content>
</tool_response>
